target_outcome: Discover traceable skill-audit capabilities, adjacent review surfaces, and raw signals for agent skills or method packages that a first-pass audit-skill search would likely miss.
why_now: Agent skill packages are spreading across runtimes and registries, so discovery needs reusable skill-audit candidates before any adoption or package-quality gate.

day1_answer.expected_source: agent skill repositories, local runtime skills, and quality-review playbooks
day1_answer.because: skill audit capabilities are more likely to appear as review skills, rubrics, package-method checklists, or adjacent code-review workflows than as a single marketplace category
day1_answer.actual_outcome: return multiple traceable skill-audit candidates and raw signals without deciding which one to use
day1_answer.timing_hypothesis: the category is active enough to have review rubrics and package-method examples, but immature enough that discovery must include adjacent review workflows
out_of_scope: installing or enabling a review skill

excluded_capabilities: generic code review with no skill/package-method rubric
security audit of source code only

lanes.target_outcome: Find skill-audit candidates for reviewing agent skills and method packages before adoption gates.
known_false_positives: generic code-review checklists without skill trigger or runtime-fit criteria
marketplace rating pages with no audit rubric

notes: Keep review skills, rubrics, and package-method workflows visible as separate candidate surfaces.
Reject generic code review unless it explicitly covers skill/package-method boundaries.
Candidate pool preserves raw signals and deferred adoption-stage boundaries only.
